prose:
Ferrocene Fukui reactivity indices were computed using GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) in the gas phase (charge 0, multiplicity 1). The xtb program evaluated the neutral molecule and its cation (+1, multiplicity 2) and anion (−1, multiplicity 2) to derive atomic Fukui indices. The iron (Fe) center was identified as the most electrophilic site with f⁺ = 0.22885456 and simultaneously the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.25938191. SCF converged for all three charge states; charge conservation checks (sum of f⁺ and sum of f⁻ each = 1.0000) confirm proper convergence. The dual descriptor at Fe (−0.030527354) indicates nucleophilic preference. Mulliken partial charges show Fe carrying +0.420 in the neutral state, +0.680 in the cation, and +0.192 in the anion. The input geometry was user-supplied (ferrocene.xyz, 21 atoms); no re-optimization was performed. Integrity status: ok (trustworthy).</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
